content_markdown:
A metal ball at a temperature of $200\,^{\circ}\text{C}$ is suspended in an evacuated container. The walls of the container are kept at a **constant** temperature of $100\,^{\circ}\text{C}$.

Which **one** of the following statements about the temperature of the ball is correct?

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a shaded circle representing a metal ball suspended inside a rectangular box representing an evacuated container. Labels indicate the vacuum, the ball at 200 degrees Celsius, and the walls at 100 degrees Celsius.

options.label: A
options.text: It will eventually reach absolute zero.

options.label: D
options.text: It will eventually reach a constant temperature between $200\,^{\circ}\text{C}$ and $100\,^{\circ}\text{C}$.

vibe_explanation: Even in a vacuum, thermal energy is transferred via radiation. The ball will emit more radiation than it absorbs until it reaches thermal equilibrium with its surroundings. Since the walls are maintained at a constant 100 degrees Celsius, the ball will continue to cool until its temperature matches that of the walls.
